radiatR 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Camera Calibration Workflow
    • Working with Loaders

    Interactive planar camera calibration

    calibration_session.Rd

    Launches an interactive graphics workflow to click calibration pattern corners in supplied frames, returning a planar camera calibration derived from the recorded correspondences.

    calibration_session(
  frames = NULL,
  pattern = c("chessboard", "charuco", "apriltag"),
  board_dims,
  square_size,
  origin = c(0, 0),
  order = c("row_major", "col_major"),
  quiet = FALSE,
  load_points = NULL,
  save_points = NULL
)

    Arguments

    frames

    Optional list of frames to annotate. Each entry may be a raster array, matrix, `as.raster()` result, or a path readable via the `png`/`jpeg` packages. Frames are only required when collecting points interactively (i.e., when `load_points` is `NULL`).

    pattern

    Calibration pattern type. Treated as a planar grid for manual annotation.

    board_dims

    Integer vector of length two giving the checkerboard square counts (rows, columns).

    square_size

    Spacing between adjacent corners in world units.

    origin

    World coordinates assigned to the top-left corner (row 0, column 0).

    order

    Traversal order for manual clicks.

    quiet

    Suppress guidance messages when `TRUE`.

    load_points

    Optional calibration point data (list, data frame, or CSV path) to use instead of interactive clicking.

    save_points

    Optional file path (CSV) at which to persist the collected point data.

    Value

    List containing a `CalModel`, the estimated intrinsics/extrinsics, per-point reprojection diagnostics, a tibble of captured points, and a convenience function `transform` that applies the calibration to a `TrajSet`.

    Contents

    Developed by John D. Kirwan.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
